observations.recommendation: 1. Municipal Mayor ~Create Inventory Committee for Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) account of ₱114,626,446.08 as of December 31, 2019 could not be ascertained due to the failure of the municipal officials to conduct annual physical inventory and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment(RPCPPE), contrary to Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units, Volume I

2. Inventory Committee ~Conduct the physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) ~Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) ~Submit the report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the audit team.

4. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). Hence, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units, Volume I and Sections 13 and 45 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units, Volume II.

6. We recommend the Management to submit the Report on Utilization, Inspection Reports, Certificates of Completion and Acceptance on Projects / Programs under the 20% component of IRA for Development Projects, in accordance to Section 2.1 of Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) – Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and Appendix 1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 9184, respectively.

9.Hence, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant direct the concerned staff to prepare and maintain schedules and subsidiary ledgers for Payable accounts pursuant to sections 111 (1) and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 or otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

15. Submit all perfected contracts, purchase Orders, and all the supporting documents within the prescribed period of five working days from the date of its issuance to afford the Auditor time to make an initial review of the contracts and purchase orders to identify to management any defects and/or deficiencies, if any, as provided under Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely review and appropriate action by the Auditor, as well as the Agency.
16. Request for technical inspection and review of contracts by the COA Technical Audit Specialist (TAS) as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001.

17. Hence, we recommend the management that at the start of the year, submit the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year with complete details and exercised full disclosure policy as required by Sections 1.1 and 2.1 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

1.10 We recommended that the LCE to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to:
1.10.1 Request copies of bank statements (BS), including debit/credit
memos (DM/CM), cashed checks, etc., from the depository bank
not later than five (5) days following the end of each month in
accordance with Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated
October 2, 1996.
1.10.2 Comply with Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011
dated October 2, 1996 and Section 74 of PD 1445 by preparing
and submitting the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements
and original copies of debit/credit memos within the required
time frame.

2.13 In light of the aforementioned, we recommended that the LCE instruct:
2.13.1 The Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of all the
LGU’s PPE and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE, which
must be submitted to the Office of the Auditor by January 31 of
each year, pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs
Volume I.
2.13.2 The Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to keep
track of their individual property cards. Afterwards, reconcile
their records, amend each property ledger card, and reflect the
right PPE account valuation in the current year's Statement of
Financial Position, in accordance with Sections 119 and 120 of
the MNGAS for LGUs Volume I and Sections 13 and 45 of the
same Manual Volume II.

3.10 Therefore, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
3.10.1 Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of
its road networks in order to ascertain the existence of the
balance of the Road Network Accounts, pursuant to Sections
V.1 and V.2 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No.
2015-008 dated November 23, 2015;
3.10.2 Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal
Accountant of the complete description and cost segregation of
the road networks to facilitate the preparation of the subsidiary
ledgers for roads and its components;
3.10.3 Require the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain
the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its
components, including Road Map, and as well as the preparation
of Report on Local Road Networks.

4.10 Thus, we recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
4.10.1 Instruct the municipal accountant to make comparable prior
years' adjustments in the books that will be closed to the
Government Equity Account; and
4.10.2 Ensure that the Municipal Accountant recognizes the correct
depreciation expense on all PPE accounts.

5.7 Given what has been said this far, we recommended that the LCE:
5.7.1 Demand that the Municipal Engineer to complete the required
Status of Work Accomplished for each infrastructure project
that management implements. This document will be verified by
the Municipality's Inspectorate Committee and serve as the
foundation for the issuance of the corresponding Certificates of
Completion and Acceptance by the head of agency.
5.7.2 Require that the Status of Work Accomplished, Inspection
Reports, and Certificates of Completion and Acceptance on
Projects / Programs under the 20% Development Fund be
submitted to the Office of the Auditor, in accordance with
Section 2.1 of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017 and Appendix 1 of
the RIRR of RA 9184, respectively.

6.4 We recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the financial
statements together with the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual
Amounts which is a part of the financial statements, in accordance with
Section 100 and Section 111 (1) of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445.

7.7 As a result, we recommended that the LCE to:
7.7.1 Instruct the Bid and Award Committee to provide the audit
team of the perfected contracts, purchase orders and all
supporting documentation within five working days from the
date of its issuance. This will allow the Auditor time to review
the contracts and purchase orders and identify to management
any defects and/or deficiencies, if any, in accordance with
Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2009-001; and
7.7.2 Submit the contract for technical inspection and evaluation by
the COA Technical Audit Specialist (TAS), to ensure the
regularity and propriety of the contracts.

3. We recommend the Management to take necessary steps for the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) as mandated under Section 39 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445 otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines; Sections 7.1, 7.2, 7.3, 7.3.1 and 7.3.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184, otherwise known as the 'Government Procurement Reform Act' and Sections 4 and 4.1 of the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) Circular No. 07-2015 dated July 16,2015, to obtain the most advantageous price for the government.

5. We recommend that appropriate disclosures should be made in the Notes to Financial Statements and that the Accountant should continue their efforts on locating the cause of discrepancy of the PPE Account to record the appropriate adjustments to reflect the correct balances, in accordance with Section 80 of the MNGAS Volume I.

7.Request the depository bank the copies of Bank Statements (BS) including debit/credit memos (DM/CM), paid checks, etc., within five (5) days after the end of each month, in accordance with Section 3.1 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

8.Prepare and submit the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and original copies of debit/credit memos within the reglementary period in pursuant to Section 74 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

10. Providing the Office of the Auditor copies complete documentary requirements such as the checks issued and other supporting documents, in accordance with Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, for the total amount of disbursements during CY 2021.
11. Practice obtaining complete documentation as enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2021, before certifying propriety of the claims/disbursements of government funds.
12. Immediately comply with the submission of the documents and reports mentioned
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 13. Impose the suspension of payment of salaries of the Accountable Officers who repeatedly failed to submit the documents and reports mentioned above within the prescribed period, as mandated in Section 122 of P.D. No. 1445; and

14. Be mindful, on pain of an administrative disciplinary action, under Section 127 of P.D. No. 1445 which states that unjustified failure of a public officer to comply with any of the provision of PD No. 1445 shall constitute neglect of duty.
18. Hence, we recommend the management to immediately comply with the submission of the quarterly and semestral reports, in accordance with Section 122 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445.

19. Therefore, we recommend the management to immediately submit the Report on Utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) for the CY 2021, pursuant to Section 4.0 of Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013.

1. We recommend to the Management to instruct the officials and personnel concerned to do, conduct and submit the reports, pursuant to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
a.) Municipal Mayor
~Create Inventory Committee for Property, Plant and Equipment
b.) Inventory Committee
~Conduct the physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE)
~Prepare the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE)
~Submit the report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the audit team.
observations.reason: No action taken by the

observations.recommendation: 2. We recommend the Management to take necessary steps for the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) as mandated under Section 39 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445 otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines; Sections 7.1, 7.2, 7.3, 7.3.1 and 7.3.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184, otherwise known as the 'Government Procurement Reform Act' and Section 4 and 4.1 of the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) Circular No. 07-2015 dated July 16,2015, to obtain the most advantageous price for the government.
observations.reason: Still no submission was made to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: 3. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Property, Plant and Equipment, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units, Volume I and Sections 13 and 45 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units, Volume II.
observations.reason: No physical inventory conducted.

observations.recommendation: 4. observations.reason: No disclosures were made to the succeeding CY.

observations.recommendation: 8. We recommend the Management to submit the Report on Utilization, Inspection Reports, Certificates of Completion and Acceptance on Projects / Programs under the 20% component of IRA for Development Projects, in accordance to Section 2.1 of Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) – Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and Appendix 1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184, respectively.
observations.reason: No submission was made to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
1.4 Therefore, we recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct
the Municipal Accountant to:
1.4.1 Request the depository bank the copies of Bank Statements (BS)
including debit/credit memos (DM/CM), paid checks, etc., within five
(5) days after the end of each month, in accordance with Section 3.1 of
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2,
1996.
1.4.2 Prepare and submit the Monthly Bank Reconciliation
Statements and original copies of debit/credit memos within the
reglementary period in pursuant to Section 74 of Presidential Decree
(PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of
the Philippines and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of Commission on Audit (COA)
Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

2.5 Hence, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant direct the
concerned staff to prepare and maintain schedules and Subsidiary
Ledgers for Payable accounts pursuant to sections 111 (1) and 114 (2)
of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 or otherwise known as the
Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

3.4 Thus, we recommend the Municipal Accountant to comply with Section
4 (6) and 39 of P.D. No. 1445 and other applicable provisions of laws,
rules and regulations by:
3.4.1 Providing the Office of the Auditor copies complete
documentary requirements such as the checks issued and other
supporting documents, in accordance with Commission on
Audit (COA) Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, for the
₱ 37,977,924.50 total amount of disbursements during CY 2021.
3.4.2 Practice obtaining complete documentation as enumerated in
COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2021, before
certifying propriety of the claims/disbursements of government
funds.

4.9 In view of the foregoing, we recommend the management to:
4.9.1 Immediately comply with the submission of the documents and
reports mentioned;
4.9.2 Impose the suspension of payment of salaries of the Accountable
Officers who repeatedly failed to submit the documents and reports
mentioned above within the prescribed period, as mandated in Section
122 of P.D. No. 1445; and
4.9.3 Be mindful, on pain of an administrative disciplinary action,
under Section 127 of P.D. No. 1445 which states that unjustified failure
of a public officer to comply with any of the provision of PD No. 1445
shall constitute neglect of duty.

5.4 Therefore, we recommend the management to:
5.4.1 Submit all perfected contracts, purchase orders, and all the
supporting documents within the prescribed period of five
working days from the date of its issuance to afford the Auditor
time to make an initial review of the contracts and purchase
orders to identify to management any defects and/or
deficiencies, if any, as provided under Section 3 of COA
Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely review and
appropriate action by the Auditor, as well as the Agency.
5.4.2 Request for technical inspection and review of contracts by the
COA Technical Audit Specialist (TAS) as required under COA
Circular No. 2009-001.

6.4 Hence, we recommend the management that at the start of the year,
submit the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to
be implemented during the year with complete details and exercised
full disclosure policy as required by Sections 1.1 and 2.1 of Commission
on Audit (COA) Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

7.4 Hence, we recommend the management to immediately comply with
the submission of the quarterly and semestral reports, in accordance
with Section 122 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise
known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

8.4 Therefore, we recommend the management to immediately submit the
Report on Utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Fund (LDRRMF) for the CY 2021, pursuant to Section
4.0 of Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 dated March
25, 2013.

5. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the list of the creditors, pursuant to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

6. We recommend the Municipal Accountant to disclose the Inter-agency Payables Account and Transfer, Assistance and Subsidy to Account in the Notes to Financial Statements, in adherence to Section 80 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) Volume I.

7. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal accountant to strictly enforce the submission of Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents to the auditor within first ten (10) days of the ensuing month, in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Account and Sections 43 and 44(k) of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units, Volume I.

9. We recommend to the Municipal Government of Buluan, Maguindanao to submit the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) for CY 2020, in accordance with Section 305 (g) of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.

3. We recommend to the Management to instruct the appropriate officials and personnel concerned to conduct and submit the reports, pursuant to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

1. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the SEF Tax personnel concerned to transfer the additional share of the one percent (1%) on the Real Property Tax totaling to ₱ 2,977,175.61 from the General Fund to the Special Education Fund.
observations.managementAction: 1.LCE instructed to transfer the SEF Tax Receivable from General Fund to the Special Education Fund.

observations.recommendation: 2.We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort in the collection of the Real Property Tax Receivable in accordance with Section 247 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: 2.LCE instructed the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort in the collection of Real Property Tax.

observations.recommendation:
4. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the following personnel:
11.1 Municipal Treasurer
11.1.1 Adhere to Section 6 of the COA Circular No. 32-382 that provides the collection of the Real Property Taxes shall be the responsibility of the city or Municipal Treasurer of the General Fund to the concerned.
11.1.2 To exert extra effort to collect the Real Property Tax through tax campaign/drive and demand letters.
11.1.3 To collect the Real Property Tax of ₱1,991,482.37.
11.2 Municipal Accountant
11.2.1 Abide to Section 272 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, which it provides that the proceeds from the additional one percent (1%) on real property occurring to the Special Education Fund (SEF) shall be automatically released to the local school board, provided that in case of provision, the proceeds shall be divided equally between the provincial and municipal school boards. Provided, however that the proceeds shall be allocated for the operation and maintenance of the public schools construction and repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment, educational research purchase of goods and periodicals and sports development as determined and approved by the local school board.
11.2.2 Transfer to the account of the Special Education Fund the amount of ₱1,318,738.42 from the General Fund.
observations.managementAction:
LCE instructed the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort in the collection of the Real Property Tax.
11.1 Municipal Treasurer
11.1.1 Adhere the Real Property Tax.
11.1.2 To exert extra effort to collect the Real Property Tax.
11.1.3 To collect the Real Property Tax of ₱1,991,482.37.
11.2 Municipal Accountant
11.2.1 Abide to Section 272 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160.
11.2.2 Transfer to the account of the Special Education Fund the amount of ₱1,318,738.42 from the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: 5. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the municipal treasurer to submit the Cashbook-LGU (Annex E-8), in accordance with Section 100 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

9.We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry to correct the Statement of Financial Position for CY 2018 in accordance with Section 58 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

10. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to drop the Land Account of ₱16,991.49 from the books of Accounts and Statement of Financial Position for CY 2018 to effect the transfer of ownership of the land from the Municipality of Buluan, Maguindanao to the Municipality of President Quirino, pursuant to Section 58 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

11.We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide for depreciation on the Land Improvement Account of ₱384,054.60, pursuant to Section 59 of PPSAS No. 17 and Section 1 (2) of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

12.We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant the following:
12.1 Abide to Section 67 of PPSAS No. 17 – Property, Plant, and Equipment and Section 9 of the Accounting Policies, Changes in Accounting Estimates and Errors.
12.2 Submit the list of the serviceable and unserviceable office equipment included in the Office Equipment Account of ₱1,390,254.79.
12.3 Submit the police spot report and pertinent documents on those unserviceable office equipment that were burned down during the fire that caught on the building of the Municipality of Buluan, Maguindanao for evaluation on the possibility of dropping from the books of accounts.
12.4 Recompute the five percent (5%) of the Residual Value and Depreciation on the typewriter that is still serviceable by applying the Straight Line Method.
12.5 Make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the financial statement.

13.We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct personnel concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension in accordance with Section 9.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

14.We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel/party concerned to hasten the completion of the Solid Waste Plan, pursuant to Section 16 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of the Philippines.

5. We recommend to the Management to instruct the officials and concerned personnel to conduct and submit the reports, pursuant to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines: a.) Municipal Mayor ~Create Inventory Committee for Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) b.) Inventory Committee ~Conduct the physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) ~Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) ~Submit the report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the audit team.
observations.managementAction: No appropriate action.
observations.reason: No Inventory Committee was created by the Local Chief Executive (LCE).

observations.recommendation: 16. We recommend to the Management to submit the Local Council for the Protection of Children to the audit Team.
observations.managementAction: No action taken. Incomplete information were submitted.
observations.reason: Incomplete information were submitted.

observations.recommendation:

Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the
Philippines.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to drop the Land Account of ₱ 16,991.49 from the books of
accounts and Statement of Financial Position for CY 2018 to effect the
transfer of ownership of the land from the Municipality of Buluan,
Maguindanao to the Municipality of President Quirino, Sultan Kudarat,
pursuant to Section 58 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise
known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Section 1 of
the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 339, entitled Creating the Municipalities of
President Quirino, Mariano Marcos and Esperanza in the Province of Sultan
Kudarat.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct

91.1 Abide to Section 67 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting

and Depreciation on the typewriter that is still serviceable by

1.The personnel in charge in the Office of the Human Resource Management should facilitate the submission of all the corresponding Statement of duties and responsibilities and corresponding accomplishment reports to the Office of the Auditor to be used as basis in verifying/comparing the above-mentioned documents to determine whether there duplication or overlapping of functions and duties which may result in the excessive hiring of said casuals
observations.managementAction: Submitted renewal of the documents.

observations.recommendation: 2. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the following municipal officials to comply with Section 100 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines: a.) Municipal Treasurer (Accountable Officer) ~Cashbook b.) Municipal Accountant ~Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements, Bank Statements, Original Paid Checks, Credit/Debit Memos and Subsidiary Ledgers
observations.managementAction: The Management had already instructed the municipal officials and municipal accountant to comply with Section 100 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and submit their respective financial reports and statements.
observations.reason: Accountable Officers was still updating his cashbook.

observations.recommendation: 3. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel concerned to transfer the fund from the General Fund to Special Education Fund.
observations.managementAction: Management had partially transfer the fund from the General Fund to Special Education Fund.
observations.reason: Real Property Tax owners did not pay their respective tax as of date.

observations.recommendation: 4. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort in the collection of the Real Property Tax in accordance with Section 247 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: The management had partially exerted effort in the collection of the Real Property Tax.
observations.reason: The Real Property Tax owners did not pay their respective tax as of date.

observations.recommendation: 6. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the list of creditors to the audit team, pursuant to Section 59 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the list.

observations.recommendation: 7. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the list of creditors under the Trust Liabilities Account totaling to ₱1,747,697.30, pursuant to Section 59 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the list.

observations.recommendation: 8. We recommend the management to submit the necessary documents on procurements in accordance with Section 4(6) of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the necessary documents on the procurements in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

observations.recommendation: 9. We recommend to the Management to submit the Solid Waste Management Plan, pursuant to Section 16 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to provide a copy of the Solid Waste Management Plan upon its completion.
observations.reason: The Solid Waste Management Plan would be provided upon completion.

observations.recommendation: 10. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the following municipal officials to comply with Section 100 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines: 10.1 Municipal Treasurer (Accountable Officer) ~Cashbook 10.2 Municipal Accountant ~Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements, Bank Statements, Original Paid Checks, Credit/Debit Memos and Subsidiary Ledgers
observations.managementAction: Personnel concerned partially complied. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant were still reconciling their records.
observations.reason: Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant were still reconciling their records; Accountable Officers were still updating their cashbook.

observations.recommendation: 11. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the following personnel: 11.1 Municipal Treasurer 11.1.1 Adhere to Section 6 of COA Circular No. 92-382 that provides the collection of the Real Property Taxes shall be the responsibility of the city or Municipal Treasurer concerned. 11.1.2 To exert extra effort to collect the Real Property Tax through tax campaign/drive and demand letters. 11.1.3 To collect the Real Property Tax of ₱1,991,482.37. 11.2 Municipal Accountant 11.2.1 Abide to Section 272 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, which it provides that the proceeds from the additional one percent (1%) on real property occurring to the Special Education Fund (SEF) shall be automatically released to the local school board, provided that in case of provision, the proceeds shall be divided equally between the provincial and municipal school boards. 11.2.2 Transfer to the account of the Special Education Fund the amount of ₱1,318,738.42 from the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Treasurer conducted collection campaign. Municipal Accountant partially complied and transferred some amounts.
observations.reason: Real Property tax payers failed to pay the RPT tax.

observations.recommendation: 12. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide for depreciation on the Other Land Improvement Account of ₱384,054.60, Road Networks Account of ₱33,536,524.13 and School Buildings Accounts of ₱10,585,997.99, pursuant to Section 59 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) No. 17 – Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
observations.managementAction: The management substantially complied and provided the required depreciation.

observations.recommendation: 13. We recommend the management to submit the necessary Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the Notes to Financial Statements, in accordance with Section 2.1 and 4.1 of COA Circular Letter No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010.

observations.recommendation: 14. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the accountable officer to remit the ₱202,956.25 to GSIS and ₱66,966.26 to HDMF for December 31, 2017, in accordance with the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR).
observations.managementAction: Management remitted the withheld mandatory deductions to their respective institutions.

municipal officials to comply with Section 100 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445,
otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines:

45.2.2 Bank Statements

45.2.4 Credit / Debit Memos

53. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the following
personnel:
53.1 Municipal Treasurer
53.1.1 Adhere to Section 6 of the COA Circular No. 32-382 that
provides the collection of the Real Property Taxes shall be the
responsibility of the city or Municipal Treasurer concerned.
53.1.2 To exert extra effort to collect the Real Property Tax through
tax campaign / drive and demand letters.
53.1.3 To collect the Real Property Tax of ₱ 1,991,482.37.
53.2 Municipal Accountant
53.2.1 Abide to Section 272 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, which
it provides that the proceeds from the additional one percent
(1%) on real property occurring to the Special Education fund
(SEF) shall be automatically released to the local school
boards, provided, that in case of provision, the proceeds shall
be divided equally between the provincial and municipal
school boards. Provided, however, that the proceeds shall be
allocated for the operation and maintenance of the public
schools construction and repair of school buildings, facilities
and equipment, educational research purchase of goods and
periodicals, and sports development as determined and
approved by the local school board.
53.2.2 Transfer to the account of the Special Education Fund the
amount of ₱ 1,318,738.42 from the General Fund.

Accounting Standards (PPSAS) No. 17 – Property, Plant and Equipment and

November 23, 2015.

observations.recommendation: 5.We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort in the collection of the Real Property Tax in accordance with Section 247 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: The Real Property Tax owners did not pay their respective tax as of date.

observations.managementAction: Still in the process of preparation.
observations.reason: The management was still in the process of preparation.

1. We recommend that Head of Agency should create a committee to conduct complete physical inventory of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) at least once a year pursuant to COA Circular No. 80-124 January 18, 1980, Section 490 of GAAM Volume 1 and Section 58 of PD 1445 and prepare the Report of the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE). Any discrepancies between physical count and inventories per books must be analyzed, investigated and corresponding course of action be taken up immediately. Report of the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) shall be submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.

2. The personnel in charge in the Office of the Human Resource Management should facilitate the submission of all Statement of duties and accomplishment reports to the Office of the Auditor to be used as basis in verifying/comparing and validating the above-mentioned documents to determine whether there duplication or overlapping of functions and duties which may result in the excessive hiring of said casuals which may be detrimental to government resources.
observations.managementAction: 1. The personnel in charge promised to submit the Statement of duties and responsibilities of the paid casuals at the Office of the Human Resource Management should facilitate the submission of all Statement of duties and Accomplishment reports to the Office of the Auditor to be used as basis in verifying/comparing and validating the above-mentioned documents to determine whether there duplication or overlapping of functions and duties which may result in the excessive hiring of said casuals which may be detrimental to government resources.
observations.reason: 1. Lack of Man Power.

observations.recommendation: 3. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the provision of Section 107. (PD 1445) and Section 6.04 COA Circular NO. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 to enable the auditor to conduct timely audit and ensure validity and accuracy of the recorded transactions.

4. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Disbursing Officer to give priority to the preparation and submission of the required reports, to facilitate timely review and verification of transaction, pursuant to Section 74 of Presidential Decree No.1445 and COA Circular No.96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

5. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to compute the residual value, depreciation and accumulated depreciation on the accounts such as Office Equipment and Motor Vehicle and to make the necessary adjusting entries: 1. For the prior year: To set-up Residual Value on Office Equipment Account of ₱1,390,254.79 and for Motor Vehicle Account of ₱1,418,180.00. Government Equity xx Accumulated Depreciation xx To compute residual value and accumulated depreciation on Office Equipment and Motor Vehicle. 2. For the current year: Depreciation – Office Equipment xx Depreciation – Motor Vehicle xx Accumulated Depreciation – Office Equipment xx Accumulated Depreciation – Motor Vehicle xx

6. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to remit the Due to GSIS account of ₱1,600,167.39 to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) and the Due to PAG-IBIG account of ₱36,033.42 to the Home Development Mutual Fund, pursuant to Section 3.4, Rule III of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of the Republic Act No.8291, otherwise known as GSIS Act of 1997 and Section 3 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No.9679, otherwise known as the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 or PAG-IBIG, respectively.
7. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension, pursuant to COA Circular No.2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

8. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the books of accounts, pursuant to Section 111 of Presidential Decree No.1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

9. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amount, pursuant to Section 109 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
